raw_consumer_statement:
While booking a hotel, I was offered a free XXXX XXXX for XXXX at XXXX XXXX in XXXX  XXXX, provided my husband and I attended a onehour presentation on XX/XX/year>. Expecting a group seminar, we were instead placed in a cubicle with a salesman named XXXX. I confirmed the onehour limit since our XXXX  daughter and her friends were waiting, but the presentation dragged on for four hours. 
XXXX pressured us to buy a vacation membership we didnt understand due to language barriers, despite repeatedly explaining we couldnt travel because of immigration issues. He offered points, cruises, discounts, and claimed it could be resold for profit. Exhausted and worried about our children, my husband left to check on them, and XXXX rushed me into signing documents I didnt fully understand. Only afterward did he mention maintenance fees and dues. 
In XX/XX/year>, I discovered a Barclays credit card had been opened in my name without my knowledge, and the membership cost was charged to it. Both XXXX and Barclays denied my refund request, citing an expired cancellation window that was never disclosed. XXXX suggested selling through XXXX XXXX, but they valued it at only {$5000.00}. 
We can not afford the debt or interest, especially after I lost my nonprofit job. The promised services do not match reality, and we have never used the membership due to its complexity and our inability to travel.
